channel.title: Dan Patrick Show (Filtered)
channel.description: Daily DP Show episodes without C&R or Best Of clips.
channel.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Matthew Stafford’s New Contract, Memorial Day Plans
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes revisit the Los Angeles Rams draft now that they have re-signed QB Matthew Stafford to a new contract. And DP considers taking prop bets on whether he will cry giving a speech at his daughter’s wedding, talks about his plans for the long weekend, and the Danettes pick out who had the best week leading up to the holiday.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Looking for Contact, Robert Horry
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes have a conversation about NBA players looking to bait opponents into fouling them and how it is affecting the style of play in the playoffs. Seven-time NBA champion Robert Horry joins Dan and discusses the performance of Knicks star Jalen Brunson, considers who, from his era, could possibly guard Victor Wembanyama, and whether it’s acceptable  to root for both the Lakers and Clippers.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Celebrity Knicks “Fans,” RIP Kyle Busch
item.description:
The New York Knicks put up a 2-0 series lead on the Cavaliers, and Dan and the Danettes discuss trying to get in on the energy at the Garden – but maybe not with the most passionate celebrity fans if they could help it. The Rams quietly extend Matthew Stafford after a wild draft season. Kyle Busch passes at the age of 41 and Dan reacts to the sudden news.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – The Hard Part Is Over, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Dan thinks the Knicks have a real shot to win the NBA Championship after finally managing to get out of the Eastern Conference. And NBA legend Reggie Miller stops by to share his thoughts on the Knicks advancing to the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – The Door Opens for LeBron to Go Home, Indy 500
item.description:
DP continues to react to the fallout of the Cavaliers failure including what it might mean for LeBron James'[ future. And he weighs in on the Indianapolis 500’s closest finish in history.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Knicks Back in the NBA Finals, Vincent Goodwill
item.description:
The Knicks rolled the Cavaliers for their 11th consecutive NBA Playoff win and completed their Eastern Conference Championship, and they now await the winner of the Thunder vs. Spurs series in the NBA Finals. NBA insider Vincent Goodwill was at the game, and he drops by to talk about the expectations for the Knicks and whether they can continue this historic run and win a ring.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 - Jamal Crawford, the NFL & Safeties
item.description:
Dan is joined by former NBA SG & NBC analyst Jamal Crawford to talk Spurs - Thunder and preview tomorrow's Game 6, the Chargers extend star safety Derwin James & thoughts on the state of the safety position in the NFL and the draft, and more!

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 - Mike Breen, Worst To Firsts
item.description:
Dan is joined by NBA play-by-play legend Mike Breen to talk about the Knicks advancing to their first Finals since 1999, teams that went from expansion to champions the fastest, callers from across the country weigh in, and more!

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 - Golden Knights to the Final, SGA's Rep
item.description:
Dan Patrick & the Danette's talk about the Las Vegas Golden Knights sweeping the Colorado Avalanche to advance to the Stanley Cup Final, thoughts on the Thunder taking Game 5 over the Spurs, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's reputation and how it will follow him, and more!

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Jalen Brunson is better than we thought, Silver doesn’t admit flopping is an issue
item.description:
Becky Hammon was among those who didn’t think Jalen Brunson could bring the Knicks as far as he has, and Dan weighs in as what the perceived talent of Brunson is and the Knicks ceiling. NBA commissioner Adam Silver waffles around the issue of flopping, and Dan thinks it’s definitely highlighted by social media. Jannik Sinner stunned at the French Open. Dan and the Danettes also discuss “Naked and Afraid” and get-in prices for the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – SEC wants to secede, Joey Votto
item.description:
Dan discusses how Isaiah Hartenstein is getting into the head of Victor Wembanyama as the Thunder-Spurs series heads to Game 6. The SEC wants to take its ball and go home, not happy with having to compete with a suddenly more equal college football landscape. Former Reds star and “Most Interesting Man in the World” Joey Votto joins Dan to discuss going to Japan to become anonymous and take sushi lessons, challenges for baseball’s ABS system and how the words “salary cap” remains a hyper-toxic issue to players.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Thunder lack personality, Mike Tirico
item.description:
Dan discusses the nature of dynasties and how we’re too quick to crown them, as the Thunder try to make the case that they could be on the way to becoming one – but they lack one important thing. NBC broadcaster Mike Tirico joins Dan to talk about the NBA playoffs so far as Knicks fans in the media are celebrating too much, and he talks about how fun it is to call basketball with the NBC crew. Dan and the Danettes talk about which athletes from this generation will have staying power as a name like Babe Ruth or Wilt Chamberlain.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Austin Rivers, Nate Bargatze
item.description:
NBA analyst Austin Rivers joins Dan again in spite of the trouble he brewed up last time; he breaks down the Spurs-Thunder series ahead of Game 7, accusations of SGA fishing for fouls, and the most intense NBA player he ever played against. Comedian Nate Bargatze stops by to talk about his new movie, swap stories of Adam Sandler with Dan and damning praise from other comedians.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Flopping to a Title, Brian Rolapp
item.description:
Dan doesn’t think Shai Gilgeous Alexander should be concerned with his reputation for flopping if it continues to bring him championships. And PGA Tour CEO Brian Rolapp joins Dan to discuss the biggest hurdles facing the Tour, getting the best talent to show up at events, and encouraging personality.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Western Conference Finals Delivering, Kyler Murray vs. JJ McCarthy
item.description:
Dan reacts to Game 6 between the Spurs and Thunder and how we’ve gotten everything we could ever want out of this series – fireworks, drama and high-level basketball. OTAs are starting and the Vikings find themselves with two quarterbacks in JJ McCarthy and Kyler Murray, and Dan can feel the drama between them simmering.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Albert Breer, Serena Williams Un-Retiring
item.description:
NFL insider Albert Breer drops by with the latest from the NFL as we approach to opening of camps. And Dan and the Danettes react to Serena Williams returning to professional tennis at the age of 44.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Lunch Thief, Sean Elliot
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss the email that came down from the head office about stealing food out of the fridge and Fritzy feels targeted. Plus, Spurs legend Sean Elliot stops by to talk about Wemby bringing San Antonio back to the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Western Conference Finals Postmortem, NBA Finals Preview
item.description:
Dan details the issues facing the Oklahoma City Thunder after they were eliminated in the Western Conference Finals and previews the matchup between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks in the upcoming NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – What the People Wanted, Jim Jackson
item.description:
Dan turns back to the NBA on the eve of the NBA Finals and is glad to see we are getting the matchup most NBA fans were hoping for. And NBA analyst Jim Jackson stops in to preview the Knicks/Spurs series.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – AJ Brown Got Traded Too, Louis Riddick
item.description:
Dan reminds us that there was another significant trade yesterday even if it was expected. And NFL insider Louis Riddick drops by to weigh in on all the movement yesterday.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Myles Garrett Goes to Hollywood, Insider Info
item.description:
Dan breaks down the Myles Garrett trade and thinks it’s truly a win/win for both teams. Plus, he and the Danettes wonder why Fritzy didn’t break the trade news considering his son is working in the Rams organization and they discuss the anatomy of a career peddling inside information like Jay Glazer’s.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Expensive Tickets, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Tickets for NBA Finals games scheduled at Madison Square Garden are through the roof and a discussion ensues about how much Dan and the Danettes might pay to see their favorite teams play for a championship. NBA legend and Hall of Famer, Reggie Miller joins the show to get everyone ready for tonight’s Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Ryen Russillo, Caleb Williams Cursed
item.description:
Longtime friend of the program, Ryen Russillo drops by to weigh in on the NBA Finals which begin tonight. Plus, Dan and the Danettes worry for Caleb Williams future after he was named as the cover athlete for Madden ’27.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – NBA Finals Preview, Aaron Donald’s Rumored Return to the Rams
item.description:
Dan is excited to see how energized New York Knicks fans are about having their squad in the NBA Finals and the buzz surrounding Victor Wembanyama’s debut on the biggest stage the NBA has to offer. And discusses the rumors and the speculation that Aaron Donald is considering a return to the Rams to play alongside Myles Garrett.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Time Will Be Kind to Russell Wilson, Channing Frye
item.description:
The former Seahawks quarterback heads for the broadcast booth and Dan thinks he will be considered for induction into the Hall of Fame in time. NBA champion Channing Frye talks about getting drafted by the Knicks, the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden, and how players have gotten into the mindset for the NBA Finals by hating the other guy’s face.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Chris Haynes, Russell Wilson Hall of Fame Push
item.description:
NBA insider Chris Haynes joins Dan to discuss how the Spurs fell apart in Game 1, how much they rely on Wembanyama’s performance and courtside altercations involving Jalen Brunson. Russell Wilson retires from football for the broadcast booth and Paulie wants to get the Pro Football Hall of Fame push started early for the former quarterback.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Knicks Steal Game 1, Fan Interference
item.description:
Dan reacts to the Knicks surging back to close and take game 1 in San Antonio. Wemby was great, but the rest of the Spurs cast performs a disappearing act. A fan invades the court and Dan and the Danettes discuss famous fan invasions and why Dan remains worried when this happens so much.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick
